messages.content: Please write a haiku to thank for my 3 years old kid

Little one so dear
Thank you for endless joy
Love you more each day
Write a haiku about my son

Bright eyes full of joy
Laughter echoes through our home
Son, my precious joy
Write a thanksgiving message for my 3 years old son in haiku format

Grateful for your love
Thankful for each precious smile
Son, our greatest gift
Write one more thanksgiving message for my 3 years old son in haiku

Thanksgiving to you,
For filling our hearts with love,
Our forever joy.
Write one more thanksgiving message for my 3 years old son in haiku

Grateful heart, my son
Thanksgiving blessings abound
Love you endlessly

Is this a haiku:

Thanksgiving to you,
For filling our hearts with love,
Our dearest son.
Yes, that is a haiku! It follows the traditional 5-7-5 syllable structure of a haiku. Well done!

Thanksgiving to you,
For filling our hearts with love,
Our most dearest son.
Yes, that is also a haiku! It has the 5-7-5 syllable structure and beautifully expresses gratitude towards your son. Well done!

how about this one?

Thanksgiving to you,
For filling our hearts with love,
Our most most most dearest son.

While your sentiment is beautiful, it doesn't follow the traditional 5-7-5 syllable structure of a haiku. In a haiku, the syllable pattern is typically 5 syllables in the first line, 7 syllables in the second line, and 5 syllables in the third line. Here's a revised version:

Thanksgiving to you,
Heart filled with love for our son,
Gratitude abounds.
how many syllables is dearest
The word "dearest" has two syllables. It is pronounced as "dee-rest."

how about this one, is it a haiku?

Thanksgiving to you,
For filling our hearts with love,
From our dearest son.
Yes, that is a haiku! It follows the 5-7-5 syllable pattern and expresses gratitude towards your son. Well done!
how many syllables does adorable have
The word "adorable" has four syllables. It is pronounced as "uh-dawr-uh-buhl."
how many syllables does "Our dearest son" have
"Our dearest son" has four syllables. It is pronounced as "Our (1) dear-est (2) son (1)."
how many syllables does "beloved" have?
The word "beloved" has three syllables. It is pronounced as "be-loved."
